titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 45

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=150 status=live modified=2017-01-30 19:28:04.071366+00 | translation moa=151 status=live modified=2016-06-07 08:55:36.837967+00

div.p:
Omnibus has litteras visuris uel audituris . Johannes de Inchesirith’ filius ⁊ heredes Duncani de Inchesirith’ Salutem .
in domino . Nouerit vniuersitas vestra me dedisse . concessisse . ⁊ hac carta mea confirmasse deo ⁊ ecclesie sancti andree
⁊ canonicis ibidem deo seruientibus ⁊ seruituris piscariam me contingentem ex aduerso piscarie prioris ⁊ conuentus
de May . videlicet illam que vocatur brekeles cum omnibus asyamentis ⁊ iuribus ad dictam piscariam spectantibus
uel de iure spectare valentibus in puram ⁊ perpetuam elemosinam . Ita libere . quiete . ⁊ honorifice . sicut aliqua elemo
sina aliquibus viris religiosis liberius . quiecius . ⁊ honorificencius in regno scocie potest dari aut concedi . Concedo eciam dictis canonicis ⁊ quibuscunque nomine ipsorum dictam piscariam piscantibus liberum transitum per ter
ram meam sine aliqua inquietacione . aut exaccione aliqua seculari . seu molestia . Et potestatem
trahendi cobellum suum ⁊ recia sua in terram siccam quociens sibi viderint expedire . ac dicta recia ibi
dem ordinare . ⁊ reficere . ⁊ desiccare . ac omnia alia faciendi que ad commoditatem ⁊ profectum
dicte piscarie poterunt proficere . sicut ego aut heredes mei aliquo tempore de dicta piscaria liberius .
⁊ commodius ad vltilitatem nostram ordinare aut facere deberemus uel possemus . In cuius rei testi
monium hinc scripto sigillum meum apposui . Testibus . Dauid fratre dicti Johannis . Domino Laurencio Muf
fechet . rectore ecclesie sancti Mathoci . Domino alexandro filio comitis de fyf’ rectore ecclesie de Ky
nule . ⁊ aliis . Datum apud Inchefyrith’ die sanctorum Nerei ⁊ achillei . Anno gracie . Mo . CCo . octogesimo tercio .

To all who shall view or hear these letters, John of Inchyra, son and heir of Duncan of Inchyra, sends Greeting in the Lord: You should all know that I have given, granted, and by this my charter made firm, to God and to the church of St Andrews, and to the the canons in that place serving God now and in the future, the fishery belonging to me opposite the fishery of the prior and convent of May, namely, that which is called ‘Brekeles’, with all easements and rights pertaining to the said fishery, or by right could pertain, in pure and perpetual alms; as freely, quietly, and honourably as any alms are able to be given or granted to any religious men freely, quietly, and honourably in the kingdom of Scotland. I also allow to the said canons, and to whomsoever is fishing the said fishery in their name, free pasage throughout my land without any discturbance, or any secular exaction or interference; and the authority to pull their boat and nets onto dry land as often as they shall see fit; and to arrange the said nets there, and to repair, and dry out, and do all the other things which they are able to do for the convenience and benefit of the said fishery, just as freely and conveniently as I or my heirs ought or could at any time arrange or do for our own benefit for the said fishery. In testimony of this matter I have therefore applied my own seal to the deed. As witnesses: David, brother of the said John; the lord Laurence Muschet, rector of the church of St Madoes; the lord Alexander, son of the Earl of Fife, rector of the church of Kinnoull; and others. Given at Inchyra, on the day of SS. Nereus and Achilleus, in the Year of Grace the twelve hundred and eighty-third.
